title: verbascum latisepalum
intro: Verbascum latisepalum, also known as the broad-leaved mullein, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow flowers and large, velvety leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Verbascum latisepalum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as an infusion or made into a salve or ointment to harness its medicinal properties.
